verses.text: Asa succeeds Abijah, and the land has rest ten years, for Asa did what was good and right in the eyes of the LORD. A godly reign brings quiet to the land. Righteousness at the top becomes peace for the people beneath.

Asa succeeds Abijah, and the land has rest ten years, for Asa did what was good and right in the eyes of the LORD. A godly reign brings quiet to the land. Righteousness at the top becomes peace for the people beneath.

Asa removes the foreign altars, high places, images, and groves, and commands Judah to seek the LORD; so the kingdom is quiet before him. Reform clears away the rivals to God. True peace follows the purging of whatever competes for the people's worship.

Asa removes the foreign altars, high places, images, and groves, and commands Judah to seek the LORD; so the kingdom is quiet before him. Reform clears away the rivals to God. True peace follows the purging of whatever competes for the people's worship.

Asa removes the foreign altars, high places, images, and groves, and commands Judah to seek the LORD; so the kingdom is quiet before him. Reform clears away the rivals to God. True peace follows the purging of whatever competes for the people's worship.

Asa builds fortified cities 'because the land had rest,' confessing they have sought the LORD and He has given them rest; he raises a mighty army. Peace is used to prepare, not to slumber. The fruit of seeking God is rest, and rest is a gift to be stewarded.

Asa builds fortified cities 'because the land had rest,' confessing they have sought the LORD and He has given them rest; he raises a mighty army. Peace is used to prepare, not to slumber. The fruit of seeking God is rest, and rest is a gift to be stewarded.

Asa builds fortified cities 'because the land had rest,' confessing they have sought the LORD and He has given them rest; he raises a mighty army. Peace is used to prepare, not to slumber. The fruit of seeking God is rest, and rest is a gift to be stewarded.

When Zerah the Ethiopian comes with a million men, Asa cries, 'Lord, it is nothing with thee to help'; he rests on God and goes out in His name. Faith owns its own helplessness and God's all-sufficiency. The prayer that leans wholly on Him cannot be refused.

When Zerah the Ethiopian comes with a million men, Asa cries, 'Lord, it is nothing with thee to help'; he rests on God and goes out in His name. Faith owns its own helplessness and God's all-sufficiency. The prayer that leans wholly on Him cannot be refused.

When Zerah the Ethiopian comes with a million men, Asa cries, 'Lord, it is nothing with thee to help'; he rests on God and goes out in His name. Faith owns its own helplessness and God's all-sufficiency. The prayer that leans wholly on Him cannot be refused.

The LORD smites the Ethiopians before Asa, and they flee while Judah gathers vast spoil. The countless host melts before God. No army is too great for the One who answers the cry of those who trust Him.
